54511271544,65535,d393fb9ab7,VENEZIA. SESTIERE DI CASTELLO,travelphotography,1799,"PONTE DEL MONDO NOVO

Il sestiere Castello è il più esteso tra i quartieri di Venezia ed è l’unico a non affacciarsi sul Canal Grande. Il suo nome deriva dalla presenza di un forte medievale che era stato costruito sull’isola di San Pietro.

Il sestiere confina direttamente con quello di Cannaregio e di San Marco. Con quest’ultimo è collegato anche tramite il famoso Ponte di Paglia che collega il molo di Piazza San Marco con la Riva degli Schiavoni.

The Castello district is the largest of the neighborhoods in Venice and is the only one that does not overlook the Grand Canal. Its name derives from the presence of a medieval fort that was built on the island of San Pietro.

The district borders directly with that of Cannaregio and San Marco. It is also connected to the latter by the famous Ponte di Paglia that connects the pier of Piazza San Marco with the Riva degli Schiavoni.

54003054643,65535,8.74E+54,Lugares emblemáticos de Venecia,N/A,35,"Venecia es una ciudad llena de historia y encanto, con numerosos lugares emblemáticos que capturan la esencia de su rica herencia cultural. Aquí tienes algunos de los más destacados:

La Plaza de San Marcos es el corazón de Venecia y uno de los lugares más icónicos de la ciudad. Aquí se encuentran la Basílica de San Marcos y el Campanile, desde donde se puede disfrutar de una vista panorámica impresionante.

El Gran Canal es la principal arteria de Venecia, serpenteando a través de la ciudad y ofreciendo vistas espectaculares de palacios históricos y puentes famosos. Un paseo en vaporetto o góndola por el Gran Canal es una experiencia imprescindible.
El Puente de Rialto es el puente más antiguo y famoso de Venecia. Con sus tiendas y su arquitectura impresionante, es un lugar perfecto para disfrutar de vistas panorámicas del Gran Canal.

El Palacio Ducal es una obra maestra del gótico veneciano y una visita obligada. Este palacio fue la residencia del Dux de Venecia y alberga impresionantes salas decoradas con obras de arte de maestros venecianosAd1.

El Puente de los Suspiros conecta el Palacio Ducal con las antiguas prisiones. 54637519706,65535,cfdec146a7,"Campos Santa Maria Formosa | Venice, Italy",N/A,93,"Campo Santa Maria Formosa
The Campo Santa Maria Formosa is vast, irregular, and full of beautiful buildings
column2: it's said to be one of the liveliest plazas on the island of Venice.

column1: It hides among the streets and alleys of the Castello district, not far from San Marco Square, which has influenced the political and religious life of this square over time.

Its charm derives in part from its proportions and, in part, from the joyful atmosphere cheered by the fruit and vegetable stalls, from the bars and cafes that offer a glimpse of the daily confusion that once characterized every field in the city.
Here we can view the Tower of St. Maria, the Catholic church from the 16th century from which the square gets it's name, and the beautiful Ruzzini Palace Hotel in rear.
You can read more about the square and it's history here:

Palais Ca’ Corner della Regina. Venise

column5: de Venise de 1834 à 1969. Depuis 2011, il est occupé par la Fondation Prada qui y organise des expositions d'art contemporain.

column1: En 2024, l'artiste suisse Christoph Büchel présente « Monte di Pietà », un environnement immersif, occupant le rez-de-chaussée, la mezzanine et le premier étage du Palais dont le thème principal est &quot
column3: . La partie principale simule un établissement prêteur sur gages qui ressemble au &quot
column5: de Venise (des photos d'archive sont exposées) mais qui serait lui même en faillite en raison de l'état d'abandon des lieux. D'autres parties de l'exposition au niveau de la mezzanine portent sur la spéculation autour des crypto-monnaies, du jeu (en référence à l'ancien casino de Venise), de la prostitution et du luxe, du commerce de marchandises et de l'or, etc.. des activités sociales qui créent de la dépendance et qui sont sources de dettes.

column1: Habitué des scandales (l'église de Venise transformée en mosquée en 2015, le bateau de migrants naufragés en 2019), l'artiste a saturé l'espace du Palais accueillant le public avec une multitude d'objets et de mobiliers pour en faire une critique du marché mondial de l'art, des pratiques sociales et des transactions financières associées, et plus largement de la société de consommation. Peu médiatisée par la Fondation Prada, cette étrange installation provoque le public mais le laisse totalement perplexe, sans repères dans un fatras gigantesque d'objets de brocante. La Fondation Prada organise une fois de plus une exposition spectaculaire mais difficile d'accès et certainement d'un coût très élevé.",08/07/2024 12:19,45.436752,12.341455,https://live.staticflickr.com/65535/53843354955_231edc9221_m.jpg,dalbera,72746018@N00,photo,"dalbera: Le bateau de migrants naufragés, installé par Christoph Büchel sur les quais de l'Arsenal, &quot

53843199664,65535,885ce0bf2a,"Le jardin conçu par Carlo Scarpa (Fondation Querini Stampalia, Venise)",dalbera venise italie fondationquerinistampalia carloscarpa bassin jardin,690,"Détail du jardin conçu par Carlo Scarpa pour la fondation Querini Stampalia à Venise
L'architecte vénitien Carlo Scarpa (1906-1978)  sur Wikipedia
Le palais Querini Stampalia datant du XVIè siècle, qui appartient à la fondation du même nom,  a été restauré par trois architectes aux styles différents :
- Carlo Scarpa (avec Giuseppe Mazzariol) a aménagé la partie basse et le jardin entre 1959 et 1963,
- Valeriano Pastor a repensé les liaisons entre les étages et entre bâtiments voisins entre 1980 et 1990
- Mario Botta a réorganisé les parties du complexe de bâtiments consacrées à l'accueil du public,  aux expositions et aux services.

La fondation Querini Stampalia est un lieu ouvert au public qui possède un musée, une librairie, un restaurant, une bibliothèque - centre de documentation, un jardin et des espaces d'expositions temporaires.

D'origine populaire, très en faveur chez les gondoliers à Venise du début xviie siècle, cette danse se répand dans toute l'Italie puis se propage ensuite en Europe. Elle est tenue pour l'une des trois danses nationales jusqu'au xviiie siècle. (extrait de Wikipedia)

53344170091,65535,4ea57f95c2,"Atmosfera gotica, chiesa Barocca - Gothic atmosphere, Baroque church",venezia venice santamariaformosa bellitalia chiesa church barocco baroque,778,"La chiesa di Santa Maria Formosa è un edificio religioso della città di Venezia: si affaccia sull'omonimo Campo (piazza). La chiesa ha origini antichissime (IX sec.) ma l'edificio venne ricostruito nel 1492 e la facciata che guarda il Campo venne realizzata nel 1604 in stile Barocco.

The church of Santa Maria Formosa is a religious building in the city of Venice (Italy): it overlooks the Campo (square) of the same name. The church has very ancient origins (9th century) but the building was rebuilt in 1492 and the façade overlooking the Campo was built in 1604 in Baroque style.

52392370976,65535,fe90fab4e0,44851,castello italy italianrepublic repubblicaitaliana europe mediterranean venice veneto venezia venesia urban city sestieri venetianrepublic venetianghetto camposantamariaformosa citysquare,383,"Campo Santa Maria Formosa a city square in Castello, one of the six sestieri of Venice, Veneto, Italy.
The square is surrounded by three canals – Santa Maria Formosa, Pestrin and Mondo Novo. In the square, there are many buildings of historical and architectural importance.
In the northern part of the square, there is the Priuli Ruzzini Palace, built as the residence of the noble Ruzzini family in the late 16th century, on a project attributed to Bartolomeo Manopola.

Next, to the northeast, there is Palazzo Morosini del Pestrin, built in the 17th century at the behest of the patrician family of Morosini del Pestrin. Between 2001 and 2009, the second floor of the palace housed the Honorary Consulate of France and the Cultural Delegation of the French Embassy in Venice.
To the east, we have the Donà Palaces (Palazzi Donà), three noble residences built between the 15th and 16th centuries for the Donà family..

Next, there is Casa Venier, built in the 15th century in Venetian-Gothic style. The palace was the residence of the Doge Sebastiano Venier, a glorious leader of Venice known for the victory of Lepanto of 1571.
Adjacent to Casa Venier, there is Palazzo Vitturi, an ancient structure built in the second half of the 13th century.

In the southern part of the square, we can find the Malipiero Trevisan Palace, the residence of the Malipiero family until the end of the 15th century, when it passed, by marriage, to the Trevisan family.

Also in the southern part of the square, there is Palazzo Querini Stampalia, built at the beginning of the 16th century for the noble Querini Stampalia family. The last member of the family, Count Giovanni, ordered that all his possessions will be donated to the city of Venice after his death.

The most important building in the square is the Church of Santa Maria Formosa. According to tradition, the church was built in 639, renovated in 864 and, after it was damaged by a fire, restored again in 1106. The church was rebuilt in 1492 by Mauro Codussi, in Renaissance style. The two facades of the church were built in the 16th century.

SANTA MARIA FORMOSA,ve venezia venice venedig venecia venise venexia serenissima veneto santamariaformosa chiesa church kirche iglesia eglise canale campanile belltower ombre shadows luce light nordest outdoor allaperto italia italy italie italien bellitalia unesco sitounesco patrimoniodellumanità canon eos600d sigma franco600d,2231,"LUCI ED OMBRE COME IL TEMPO CHE VIVIAMO....

La chiesa è tra le otto chiese più antiche di Venezia. Si racconta che venne fondata nel 639 da San Magno, vescovo di Oderzo, sul luogo in cui gli apparve la Vergine come donna formosa, ovvero bellissima. L’edificio attuale è uno dei capolavori di Mauro Codussi (1492-1504) che ripropone l’impianto bizantino a croce greca razionalizzandolo in forme rinascimentali. La facciata principale venne eretta nel 1542 per celebrare il grande capitano da mar Vincenzo Cappello, del quale è presente sopra il portale la statua.
LIGHTS AND SHADOWS LIKE THE TIME WE LIVE ....

The church is among the eight oldest churches in Venice. It is said that it was founded in 639 by San Magno, bishop of Oderzo, on the place where the Virgin appeared to him as a curvy, or rather beautiful, woman. The current building is one of Mauro Codussi's masterpieces (1492-1504) who re-proposes the Byzantine Greek cross layout, rationalizing it in Renaissance style. The main facade was erected in 1542 to celebrate the great captain da mar Vincenzo Cappello, whose statue is present above the portal.

50914449111,65535,e82a9c26c7,38534,castello italy italianrepublic repubblicaitaliana europe mediterranean venice veneto venezia venesia urban city sestieri venetianrepublic church chapel worship religion christ christian christianity santamariaformosa renaissance maurocodussi sanmagno bishopofoderzo stbarbarapolyptych palmatheelder conceptionchapel triptych madonnaofmisericordia bartolomeovivarini oratory madonnawithchild stdominic giambattistatiepolo lastsupper leandrobassano,1719,"Santa Maria Formosa a church in Castello, one of the six sestieri of Venice, Veneto, Italy.

It was erected in 1492 under the design by Renaissance architect Mauro Codussi. It lies on the site of a former church dating from the 7th century, which, according to tradition, was one of the eight founded by San Magno, bishop of Oderzo. The name &quot
column3: relates to an alleged appearance of the Holy Virgin disguised as a voluptuous woman.

column1: The plan is on the Latin cross, with a nave and two aisles. The two façades were commissioned in 1542, the Renaissance-style one facing the channel, and 1604, the Baroque one facing the nearby square. The dome of the church was rebuilt in after falling during an earthquake in 1688.

The artworks in the interior include the St. Barbara polyptych by Palma the Elder, one of his most celebrated works. The Conception Chapel houses a triptych of Madonna of Misericordia by Bartolomeo Vivarini, while in the Oratory is the Madonna with Child and St. Dominic by Giambattista Tiepolo. Also, within the church is the Last Supper by Leandro Bassano.

49932726022,65535,f5f2b8899e,*Streets of Ocean,venice canal blue green orange ocean water people gondola beautiful serene rich deep color joy happy amazing view,5960,"Sometimes there is a photograph that demands to be taken and will not wait another second!  So I didn't.

When the sun is high and the canals are narrow, there is always a gondola or two gliding under the bridges and through the painted streets of Venice.  I can still hear the sighing of the oars swishing into the sparkling water as a gondolier sings an enchanted song for lovers and passers by.
In this city we are all lovers whether we are alone or with someone else.

48838996392,65535,ebd15fd4ee,Chiesa di Santa Maria Formosa_00210,внебо architecture belltower bodiesofwater church italy religion temple tourism travel venice water canal архитектура венеция вода водоем италия канал колокольня путешествия религия церковь 34ve italia,992,"The church of Santa Maria Formosa was erected in 1492 according to the design of the Renaissance architect Mauro Codussi. It lies on the site of a former church dating from the 7th century, which, according to tradition, was one of the eight churches founded by San Magno, bishop of Oderzo. The name &quot
column3: relates to an alleged appearance of the Holy Virgin disguised as a fashionable woman.

column1: Это первая церковь в Венеции, посвященная Деве Марии. Первое упоминание касается восстановления в 1060 году, было еще одно или два перестроения перед тем как церковь была окончательно построена Мауро Кодусси в 1492 году на фундаменте ранней церкви, которая к тому времени была почти в руинах. Для внешних фасадов, заказанных семьей Каппелло, были выбраны два разных стиля: фасад в классическом стиле, выходящий на канал, построенный в 1542 году, и фасад в стиле барокко, выходящий на небольшую Кампо в 1604 году.",10/09/2019 16:38,45.437183,12.340561,https://live.staticflickr.com/65535/48838996392_ebd15fd4ee_m.jpg,ValKamch,143593890@N07,photo,"Gérard & Françoise: <b>Vue dans le groupe

Le lustre/ chandelier Rezzonico en verre de Murano date de 1870.

La fondation Querini Stampalia est un lieu ouvert au public qui possède un musée, une librairie, un restaurant, une bibliothèque - centre de documentation, un jardin et des espaces d'expositions temporaires.

48180079217,65535,c7ce04cac8,"Le jardin de Carlo Scarpa (Fondation Querini Stampalia, Venise)",dalbera venise italie fondationquerinistampalia carloscarpa bassin jardin,1035,"Bassin dans le jardin conçu par Carlo Scarpa pour la fondation Querini Stampalia à Venise
Le palais Querini Stampalia datant du XVIè siècle, qui appartient à la fondation du même nom,  a été restauré par trois architectes aux styles différents :
- Carlo Scarpa (avec Giuseppe Mazzariol) a aménagé la partie basse et le jardin entre 1959 et 1963,
- Valeriano Pastor a repensé les liaisons entre les étages et entre bâtiments voisins entre 1980 et 1990
- Mario Botta a réorganisé les parties du complexe de bâtiments consacrées à l'accueil du public,  aux expositions et aux services.

32354329028,4880,6ef4c5521f,"Cat among the pigeons, Venice",italia italy venezia venice,457,"Cat among the pigeons, Venice, 18 Nov 2018
Never seen anything like this before.
The pigeons have individually worked out a safe distance from the cat. No doubt complex maths involving pigeon reaction time  plus take-off time  versus cat acceleration time.
All remained in place until somebody walked through the centre of the picture and all players left the scene.

32844288496,2633,34a7704eff,VENEZIA. CAMPO DI SANTA MARIA FORMOSA,venezia venedig venice venecia veneto italia italy italie italien bellitalia notturno serenissima campo santamariaformosa parrocchiale chiesa campanile leggenda doge mercato amici canon orablu luna luce sigma eos600d franco600d,3386,"NOTTURNO SULLA PARROCCHIALE.

La leggenda narra che la Beata Vergine apparve in questo luogo al vescovo S. Magno comandandogli di erigere una chiesa in suo nome. Questa nuova chiesa fu dedicata alla Purificazione della Beata Vergine, e il popolo la chiamò con il nome di S.M. Formosa in memoria alle forme prosperose con le quale la Vergine apparì. La leggenda è sorta nel Trecento e se ne ha documentazione per la prima volta nel 1060. I &quot
column3: (fabbricatori di casse che servivano per le spedizioni di merci o per contenere il corredo delle spose novelle) avevano la loro scuola di devozione, sacra a S. Giuseppe, presso la chiesa di S.M. Formosa riportando una vittoria nel giorno della Purificazione di Maria Vergine, chiesero al doge e alla Signoria di recarsi ogni anno in visita alla chiesa . In occasione di tale visita il doge veniva omaggiato dal pievano di S.M. Formosa di due cappelli di carta o di paglia dorata, due fiaschi di malvasia con sopra due aranci : questi doni furono la conseguenza della risposta assai scherzosa che il doge diede alla loro richiesta di pellegrinaggio: &quot
column4: E se stesse per piovere? E se avessimo sete?&quot
column5: e i casselleri prontamente gli risposero: &quot
column6: Noi vi daremo cappelli da coprirvi e vi daremo da bere&quot

column1: Nel campo di S.M. Formosa si tennero numerose feste: ricordiamo la festa notturna con grandiosi fuochi d’artificio, e nell’anno seguente una magnifica gara coi tori ed orsi in onore di Ferdinando Gran Principe di Toscana, festa che fu funestata però dalla morte di due donne a seguito di un crollo di un’altana.

25644226295,1591,51b9be9e9e,VENEZIA - Old Well,italien venice italy vacances italia urlaub brunnen well vera venezia venedig italie venetia put 2015 vacanta concediu,442,"Venice was surrounded by salt water but didn’t have drinking water, so some wells (“vera”) were built  to collect rainwater.

“Vera” is a typical Venetian word that means the visible stone, the one that covers the well itself. At the beginning, to cover the wells were used recycled materials, taken from the ruins of ancient Altino, and in fact the oldest wells have “vere” made with some portions of capitals and columns sections.
Many of these “vere” show effigy of the family that has built the well. The largest one is located in Campo San Polo and measures 320 cm in diameter.
In XIX century the census counted about six thousand wells, but after 1884, after the aqueduct construction, many wells were destroyed, especially the brick ones.
Today Venice has about 600 wells, of which none are in use.

Die Gesamtflaeche Venedigs betraegt 414,6 kmÂ², davon entfallen 257,7 kmÂ² auf Wasserflaechen. Am 31. Dezember 2013 zaehlte die Stadt 264.534 Einwohner, davon 181.883 in den Stadtteilen auf dem Festland, 58.901 im historischen Zentrum und 29.674 innerhalb der Lagune. In der Lagune befinden sich 118 Inseln. Sie erstreckt sich Ã¼ber etwa 50 km zwischen den Muendungen der Fluesse Adige (Etsch) im Sueden und Piave im Norden in die Adria.

11627994804,5484,d8d4dc6e1b,IAEV - Ponte del Paradiso 1890(-1900) - 2013,venezia santamariaformosa calledelparadiso pontedelparadiso pontedeipreti iantichieditorivenezia,278,"Nel Sestiere di Castello, in parrocchia di Santa Maria Formosa, c'è questo ponte dal bellissimo nome di cui nessuno sa però perché (come anche della calle che vi porta). La foto a sinistra è stata scattata tra il 1890 e 1900 la foto a destra negli ultimi giorni di dicembre 2013. La più grande differenza è senza dubbio il ponte. Centoventi anni fa era in ferro (della fonderia Neville) ed unito direttamente al vicinissimo ponte dei Preti
column2: entrambi disgiunti e rifatti in pietra durante i primi due decenni del novecento. Ma anche molte finestre, e porte, e case, si sono modificate nei decenni. La struttura più leggera e deperibile di tutte, l'altana, è stata ripetutamente rifatta quasi uguale.

10659582823,5513,013f55ae2d,1001,italien venice italy square italia quadro venezia venedig quadrato quadrat cuadrado carrée quadratique quadrique cuadrático quadratico,416,"Rio del Mondo Novo

Le rio del Mondo Novo (Nuovo en italien) est un canal de Venise dans le sestiere de Castello. Le rio del Mondo Novo a une longueur de 246 mètres. Il prolonge les rio de la Canonica et de San Zulian vers le nord-est, tourne ensuite vers le nord-ouest au carrefour avec les rii de S.M. Formosa et de San Zaninovo pour rejoindre le rio del Piombo. Le nom proviendrait d'un magasin ou marchand de vin proche qui, vers 1590, s'appela el Mondo Novo. Le Mondo Novo réfère au nouveau monde, découvert à la fin du xve siècle.",29/10/2013 19:49,45.436812,12.340629,https://live.staticflickr.com/5513/10659582823_013f55ae2d_m.jpg,visualimpakkt,50224162@N06,photo,N/A

8130363053,8045,d4327a01a2,Anime pesanti,morte anima venezia viaggio isoladisanmichele isoladeimorti rugagiuffa fondamentarimedio,325,"L'imbarcazione avanzava veloce e senza indugio nel canale di Fondamenta Rimedio.
Non curante dei passanti di Ruga Giuffa fendeva determinata l'acqua salata e fredda,
e con onde violente spingeva in là le nere gondole adornate di drappi rossi e antichi stemmi.
Sembrava che fosse la morte stessa, risoluta ed implacabile, a condurre quel viaggio
frettoloso che dalla terra e attraverso il mare conduceva fino all'Isola dei Morti.
Capì subito che il giudizio attendeva un'anima... ma non una qualunque, una pesante.

8030088640,8444,8fcae798c3,"Venedig, Fondazione Querini Stampalia, Judith von Vincenzo Catena / Giuditta",venice judith venezia venedig giuditta fondazionequerinistampalia sestierecastello vincenzocatena,6607,"Im Alten Testament wird berichtet, dass die Stadt Bethulia  von den Assyrern so lange belagert worden war, dass die Einwohner bereit waren, sich zu unterwerfen. Die reiche und auffallend schöne Witwe Judith beschloss jedoch, sich in das Lager des Feindes zu begeben, um ihr Volk zu retten. Sie ging zu Holofernes, dem Anführer der Assyrer, und gab vor, mit ihm die Kapitulation von Bethulia aushandeln zu wollen. Holofernes verliebte sich in die schöne Frau und als ihn nach einem üppigen Bankett und reichlichem Alkoholgenuss der Schlaf übermannte, schlug ihm Judith mit einem Schwert den Kopf ab.

La culture du coton est au coeur de l'économie de pays comme l'Ouzbékistan, son caractère intensif développé à l'époque soviétique a entrainé la catastrophe écologique de la mer d'Aral. En nécessitant beaucoup d'eau, cette culture a augmenté la salinisation des terres du pays en raison de la baisse du débit des grands fleuves.

La présence soviétique a mis fin à l'archaïsme dans lequel vivaient les pays d'Asie centrale, elle a fortement limité sinon interdit les pratiques religieuses tout en supprimant le port du voile. Les soviétiques ont envoyé femmes et enfants à l'école. Il faut rappeler que l'esclavage a existé jusqu'en 1920 dans certaines régions d'Ouzbékistan.

6244941980,6151,4f63cf4bee,Pavillon d'Asie centrale (54ème biennale de Venise),venice biennale venise italie dalbera biennaledevenise pavillondasiecentrale,1303,"Mutation

A travers les métamorphoses de la représentation de Lénine, l'artiste entend montrer les révolutions idéologiques et leurs cortèges iconographiques associés que les pays centre-asiatiques ont subis au cours des siècles passés. A ce sujet, un des plus étranges bouleversements est la célébration de Tamerlan, un conquérant parmi les plus sanguinaires, érigé en héros de l'Ouzbékistan au moment de la chute des icones du Communisme : Marx, Lénine, et avant eux Staline. L'oeuvre dépasse néanmoins le contexte centre-asiatique pour interroger les formes liées à l'idéologie qui se succèdent au cours des siècles dans tous les pays du monde.
Pavillon collectif d'Asie centrale : Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Uzbekistan

6244430015,6094,4514324509,Pavillon d'Asie centrale (54ème biennale de Venise),venice biennale venise italie linguafranca dalbera biennaledevenise pavillondasiecentrale,2480,"Le palais d'accueil du pavillon d'Asie centrale
La thématique de cette exposition porte sur les systèmes et langages de communication à l'image de ceux à l'origine de la &quot
column3: , cette sorte d'espéranto qui servait autrefois aux échanges commerciaux dans le monde méditerranéen. Les pays de l'Asie centrale en tant que lieux de passage depuis l'Antiquité et de commerce (route de la soie) entre l'Orient et l'Occident sont depuis des millénaires au carrefour des grandes civilisations de l'Humanité.

column1: Plus récemment, après la période soviétique où la religion islamique a été pratiquement interdite, ils ont connu un retour à ces valeurs religieuses tout en subissant des régimes politiques autoritaires et autocratiques calqués sur les modèles de l'époque soviétique. Les mêmes dirigeants autrefois communistes se réclament maintenant de l'Islam. Mais les changements affectent tous les aspects de la vie quotidienne de ces peuples
column2: un exemple est le changement d'alphabet imposé par la mondialisation car certains textes sur les affiches (par ex. l'affiche de l'exposition) sont écrits en alphabet cyrillique et latin, les anciens ne s'y retrouvent plus.

column1: L'identité de ces populations apparaît en perpétuelle mutation, et il en est de même des repères historiques. Ainsi en Ouzbékistan, les statues de Tamerlan, ce tyran sanguinaire sans lien avec les racines nomades des Ouzbeks, ont remplacé celles de Lénine en quelques mois au moment de la chute du communisme et de l'indépendance. On peut douter du sens d'un tel choix dans un pays qui s'ouvre au tourisme international grâce à un riche patrimoine islamique mais qui veut vivre à l'heure de la mondialisation.

Dans un tel contexte mouvant, on comprend le sens de la thématique retenue pour l'exposition, elle est ancrée au plus profond de l'histoire des pays islamiques d'Asie centrale. Les artistes centre-asiatiques ont répondu à l'invitation avec finesse et cohérence mais il n'est pas certain que le public de la biennale en ait toujours perçu les enjeux artistiques et culturels sous-jacents, faute peut-être de bien connaître ces pays.
Pavillon collectif d'Asie centrale : Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Uzbekistan

La culture du coton est au coeur de l'économie de pays comme l'Ouzbékistan, son caractère intensif développé à l'époque soviétique a entrainé la catastrophe écologique de la mer d'Aral. En nécessitant beaucoup d'eau, cette culture a augmenté la salinisation des terres du pays en raison de la baisse du débit des grands fleuves.

La présence soviétique a mis fin à l'archaïsme dans lequel vivaient les pays d'Asie centrale, elle a fortement limité sinon interdit les pratiques religieuses tout en supprimant le port du voile. Les soviétiques ont envoyé femmes et enfants à l'école. Il faut rappeler que l'esclavage a existé jusqu'en 1920 dans certaines régions d'Ouzbékistan.

6244941980,6151,4f63cf4bee,Pavillon d'Asie centrale (54ème biennale de Venise),venice biennale venise italie dalbera biennaledevenise pavillondasiecentrale,1303,"Mutation

A travers les métamorphoses de la représentation de Lénine, l'artiste entend montrer les révolutions idéologiques et leurs cortèges iconographiques associés que les pays centre-asiatiques ont subis au cours des siècles passés. A ce sujet, un des plus étranges bouleversements est la célébration de Tamerlan, un conquérant parmi les plus sanguinaires, érigé en héros de l'Ouzbékistan au moment de la chute des icones du Communisme : Marx, Lénine, et avant eux Staline. L'oeuvre dépasse néanmoins le contexte centre-asiatique pour interroger les formes liées à l'idéologie qui se succèdent au cours des siècles dans tous les pays du monde.
Pavillon collectif d'Asie centrale : Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Uzbekistan

5390974920,5135,cbe354471e,Santa Maria Formosa church,world street trip travel venice sea italy tourism church water beautiful architecture photoshop wonderful square la canal nice fantastic nikon perfect italia republic tour superb awesome famous sigma lagoon charm tourist journey stunning excellent romantic gondola lovely incredible 1020 venezia hdr breathtaking rialto adriatic turism d300 turist giudecca worldfamous serenissima photomatix vaporeto brathtaking slod300,327,"Venice, Italy (Venezia in Italian) is one of the most interesting and lovely places in the world. This sanctuary on a lagoon is virtually the same as it was six hundred years ago, which adds to the fascinating character. Venice has decayed since its heyday and is heavily touristed (there are slightly more tourists than residents), but the romantic charm remains.

The Most Serene Republic of Venice dates back to 827, when a Byzantine Duke moved its seat to what is now known as the Rialto, and for the following 970 years, prospered on trade and under the rule of a Roman-style Senate headed by the Doge. Alas in 1797, the city was conquered by Napoleon, a blow from which the city never recovered. The city was soon merged into Austria-Hungary, then ping-ponged back and forth between Austria and a nascent Italy, but Venice is still a monument to the glory days of the Renaissance, and historical culture still throbs powerfully in the old Italians' veins.",26/01/2011 19:32,45.437332,12.340811,https://live.staticflickr.com/5135/5390974920_cbe354471e_m.jpg,Uros P.hotography,36557347@N00,photo,N/A

5389323497,5252,ce9b143fd1,Venice Euro Saving Tip No. 1,venice italy 2011,841,"Let's start with the buzz.  In Venice, you'll likely want to drink wine.  Here is one of two locations I know where you can bring in a bottle, any bottle, and this guy will fill it from his wonderful set up of bushel baskets, rubber hoses and casks.  Just take your pick.  Here, (the lens still a bit misty from coming inside from the cold), he holds up our 2 liter Aldi water bottle, just filled with the Malbek for 3 Euro.  You get the inexpensive wine (which ain't bad) and you get to interact with this scene.

You can find the precise location of this shop on the Flickr mapping feature.  It is just off the Campo Maria Formosa, over the first canal bridge in the direction of Piazza San Marco, on the left. The other shop is on the Fondamenta della Misericordia, over in Sestiere Carnereggio, an area of Venice generally ignored by tourists and so of course I recommend you stroll over there.",04/01/2011 18:11,45.436752,12.340553,https://live.staticflickr.com/5252/5389323497_ce9b143fd1_m.jpg,CHEEZMAN,51041866@N00,photo,"werner boehm *: very nice scene

5160251259,4145,429bd7887a,Moon over church,venice italy veneza italia venecia venezia itália canonefs1022mmf3545usm mywinners canoneos40d,4228,"Santa Maria Formosa church

Tradition has it that the Virgin Mary appeared to St Magnus, Bishop of Oderzo, in the form of a buxom (formosa in Italian) woman and told him to build her a church under a white cloud. And so this, the first church in Venice dedicated to the Virgin Mary, was built, some time in the 7th Century by the Tribuno family. It was rebuilt in the 11th Century. It was rebuilt again in 1492 by Mauro Codussi who kept to the dimensions of the foundations of the (by then almost ruined) 11th Century church.

The church was visited by the doge and a procession of twelve young girls every 2nd of February. This procession of 'Marias' was to commemorate the rescue of the brides abducted by pirates from Istria and Trieste from San Pietro di Castello some time in 944. Santa Maria Formosa being the centre for the guild of casselleri (casemakers) who carried out the rescue and requested an annual visit from the doge in reward.

The current church was planned by Codussi but the exterior was completed after his death in 1504 by unknown hands. The façade onto the rio was erected in 1542 and commemorates Vincenzo Cappello, a sea captain who defeated the Turks. The façade onto the campo (maybe by Smeraldi) was completed in 1604 and contains portraits of other members of the Cappello family. These façades were paid for by...well, you guess. They were restored by Venice in Peril in the mid-90s. The interior was renovated by merchant Torrino Tononi in 1689. The dome was repaired in 1668 after an earthquake and in 1921 following a hit during Austrian bombing in 1916.

A Gondola is a Venetian rowing boat. It is propelled by a gondolier.

The next canal is the Rio di Santa Maria Formosa.

As you can see, it got busier!

Today I managed to apply to another grad school and got a lot of stuff done related to that.  Tomorrow I'm going to do some more, and go to the gym and hopefully watch some stuff on TV related to Barack Obama's 1st anniversary as President.  I'm assuming there'll be some stories on that.",29/08/2008 4:44,45.437004,12.341455,https://live.staticflickr.com/4031/4288972665_85da2d63f2_m.jpg,Loops666,28195868@N00,photo,LanieB1966: Nicely captured | Je répondrai: A very interesting perspective...

4288569124,4033,7fb13af85b,IMG_8402.JPG,morning venice shadow summer sky italy woman man men tower church public canon buildings bag square eos kid women italia child stroller august umbrellas 2008 venezia sooc 450d camposantamariaformosa canonrebelxsi,753,"The next morning, I went out by myself to take pictures, since I didn't want to sit in the hotel room doing nothing.  The hotel room, by the way, where I was sitting when they announced that Sarah Palin was going to be the Republicans' Vice-Presidential candidate.  I remember watching the news on the TV which was hung from the ceiling in the south-east corner of the room.  It was BBC.

Anyway, I wandered around, knowing that I'd probably get lost...and I did!  However, I did get 16 pictures in, including several of this square.  I tried to get a more panoramic shot, but it wouldn't happen.  I tried to stick two shots together, but it looks like I was actually a little bit farther forward on this shot than on the previous one, and my software doesn't allow rotation of a selection without rotating the whole stitch project...so...one of these days I'll get Photoshop and a new computer, haha.

After a few minutes of trying to orient myself, I did find my way back to the hotel.",29/08/2008 4:41,45.437719,12.340768,https://live.staticflickr.com/4033/4288569124_7fb13af85b_m.jpg,Loops666,28195868@N00,photo,"Eridony (Instagram: eridony_prime): Beautiful city. I'm surprised this square looks so empty, was it very early? | Loops666: According to my camera, it was taken at 10:41am.  I remember it feeling early, too... | CTS56: Nice picture, you must have been on a slow day to get a pic like that. | Trudy Veitch: Love these Venice photos.  Good luck with the new computer!

4126909893,2624,9b52f55a18,Gondolieri – is this The Grand Canal ??,old venice italy water buildings geotagged canal grand tourists gondola venezia hdr the gondolieri janusz leszczynski bestcapturesaoi geo:lat=45436782 geo:lon=12340616 004544,3978,"Gondolieri – is this The Grand Canal ? If you wanted to see The Grand Canal Madam than – Yes IT  IS !
Just one of the images from thousands from Venice and I never took a ride on gondola  - not much of my thing … I was way more happy with my backpack and tripod going where the camera was pointing.
… and this is not The Grand Canal … it's one of lesser grand but still the canal .

And a little announcement , I just got awarded 1st place in Delta Photo Club's – Print Challenge. The competition was between 6 clubs with 60 prints. What make me even more happy our club ( Surrey Photography Club ) won overall and  got 1st &amp

column1: See where this picture was taken , Venice - Italy [?]",25/09/2009 11:29,45.436782,12.340616,https://live.staticflickr.com/2624/4126909893_9b52f55a18_m.jpg,janusz l,44493439@N00,photo,"janusz l: Here is the winning image - entered under the name “ Locomotion “ ( I still can't believe it )
Just a bit about this image.

It is hand-held 3-image HDR of Royal Hudson  taken last May in Langley . I usually would take up to 14 images for HDR every 2/3 stops but this one only 3  ( +2 , 0 , -2 ) and while playing with HDR – Photomatix, I just pushed temperature slider all the way to the right and it just something new showed up. Suddenly from cold blue steel it turned to bronze “ social realism “ statue.
Unfortunately I had to work and could not attend the competition ( sucks ! )

Lonely boat awaiting the owner - maybe - to take him on a trip.",05/01/2009 13:49,45.437166,12.340961,https://live.staticflickr.com/3345/3229745274_b02cba9b21_m.jpg,FiumeSenzaFine,33364386@N04,photo,"fuzzy increase: I like this... | Omar Cirotto: Scatto meraviglioso!

E bellissimo titolo, per quella che secondo me è una delle tue più riuscite fotografie. La suggestione del titolo e le geometrie dello scatto, coi suoi chiaroscuri e riflessi, crea una perfetta alchimia che mai come in questo caso rende foto e titolo elementi indissolubili nello sforzo artistico dell'autore.

Credo sia, per composizione e taglio del soggetto, la miglior fotografia fin qui pubblicata. Sicuramente una delle più ispirate. L'inseguirsi, sull'acqua quasi immobile del canale, delle ombre e convergenze è qualcosa di struggente e lascia addosso una sensazione lieve, quasi rarefatta. Un senso di malinconia che si stempera nella dolcezza del presentimento che qualcosa, prima o poi, avverrà comunque.
Impeccabile infine la postproduzione, che risalta ancora di più, se possibile, la potenza suggestiva del titolo. | FiumeSenzaFine: Thank you...

3211538305,3440,86eb510259,"Campo Santa Maria Formosa, Venice, Italy",santa venice italy italia maria campo formosa venezia castello sestiere,5729,"Campo Santa Maria Formosa, Sestiere of Castello, Venice, Italy
Tradition says that the construction of this church has been due to an apparition of Our Lady as a shapely (formosa in Italian) woman to Saint Magno in the seventh century.

Building has been rebuilt in the 1492 by architect Codussi. Church is really particular because of the two facades it owns, one facing the channel and one facing the campo (square in Venice), both wanted by the Captain Cappello's family, whose member fought and won the Turks with the fleet of Serenissima.
The bell tower has a baroque age (finished in the 1688).

Church, that gave hospitality to the Scuola dei Casselleri (nuptial case maker) for long time, received the annual visit by the Doge till the end of 1797. During this event honouring the merit of Casselleri in occasion of the Slavic's Abduction in the 944, when some Slavics tempt to kidnap many young Venetian woman, the Doge received a straw hat as a gift.

Building has a Latin cross plan with three naves and transept with groin vault and presbitery. In the interior are kept works by Palma il Giovane, by Vivarini and, in the chapel of the Scuola dei Bombardieri that here had the headquarters, a polittico by Jacopo Palma il Vecchio (1480-1510) representing Santa Barbara con i Santi Sebastiano e Antonio Abate and La pietà tra i Santi Domenico e Giovanni Battista.",16/04/2004 13:11,45.437437,12.340886,https://live.staticflickr.com/3440/3211538305_86eb510259_m.jpg,Eustaquio Santimano,25509772@N00,photo,N/A

Santa Maria Formosa is a church in Venice, northern Italy. It was erected in 1492 under the design by Renaissance architect Mauro Codussi.
